meta.name: Kitani Islam
meta.shortBio: 9+ years of software engineering experience across North Africa, Europe, and Asia, I build Web/Mobile products and backend systems. empowering them with AI capabilities.

services.title: Services I Provide
services.description: Promotional features that reflect how I can help across product, engineering, AI, and design.

items.title: Web / Mobile
items.text: I build web and mobile experiences that are practical, fast, and ready for real users.

items.title: Backends and APIs
items.text: I design backend systems and API layers that keep products stable, scalable, and easier to extend.

items.title: AI Integration / RAG
items.text: I can help add AI-powered features and lightweight RAG workflows into products when they make sense. I position this as practical integration, not deep AI research.

items.title: Good Eye for Design
items.text: I care about product feel, visual clarity, and details that make interfaces look polished and easier to trust.

caseStudies.title: Featured Product Work
caseStudies.description: A closer look at one product where brand, planning flows, and cross-platform delivery had to work together.

items.name: Rep&Sets
items.impact: A cross-platform training product for coaches and athletes, connecting routines, matrixes, exercises, videos, playback, and progress through one clear system.

items.name: Fit2Go
items.impact: A prepared-meal ordering product where I was brought in to improve and gamify the plan-selection flow around goals, allergies, preferences, and weekly meal quantity.

items.role: Otonoco AI - Lead Software Engineer (Kuala Lumpur)
items.details: Leading a fast-growing startup team building an AI product for regulatory monitoring in banking and insurance, with ownership of technical decisions, release planning, task assignment, and turning user feedback into product priorities.

Led the company shift to JS-based web/mobile services, mentored .NET/C# developers through workshops and coding sessions, defined engineering workflows, and supported the public launch of CheckD Wallet with Dataswyft.
Led product delivery across mobile and web initiatives, including client onboarding flows, API optimization, data security and authorization, task ownership, and code reviews.
Co-founded Spon.Social and completed the 6-week Antler pre-seed program, validating product direction and business model while navigating early-stage monetization challenges.

As CTO and co-founder, designed relational database architecture, built and secured backend services, optimized mobile app performance, and shaped a fast 60-second matching experience for nearby activity discovery.

items.role: Trienpont International - Consulting Senior Full Stack Developer (Bangkok)
items.details: Led and mentored junior developers through pair programming, technology selection by requirements, workflow standards, database design, mobile delivery, and close collaboration with design for better UI/UX.

items.role: RiverDone Digital - Fullstack React/React Native Developer (UK, Remote)
items.details: Delivered and optimized products for clients including Walmart, TATA, Finimize, and Juiceplus, with responsibilities covering refactoring, troubleshooting, CI/CD automation, native module integration, planning, and building apps from scratch.
Led the web team on an Uber-like platform, managed frontend delivery, collaborated with design and mobile teams, shaped API structures, and introduced automation workflows.
Supported a blockchain startup by accelerating delivery through backend/frontend integration support, third-party exchange API integration, and targeted code refactoring.

items.role: Adsumnetworks - R&D Full Stack Developer (UK, Remote)
items.details: Co-founded an IoT initiative in intelligent transportation, leading software development across cloud, web/mobile apps, APIs, and data storage, including field testing workflows recognized by Catapult Network UK.

items.description: Specialized in Software Engineering and Computer Systems.

items.title: 1st Place - NASA App Challenge 2016
items.description: Won first place in the NASA App Challenge competition, developing an innovative solution for pandemic evacuation planning.

items.title: Catapult UK Recognition

technologiesSection.title: Tools I Build With
technologiesSection.description: Production tools across frontend, mobile, backend, data, cloud, and delivery workflows.

repnsets.name: Rep&Sets
repnsets.tagline: Training structure that looks as sharp as it performs.
repnsets.summary: Rep&Sets is a training product for coaches and athletes, connecting routines, matrixes, exercises, videos, and progress across web and mobile in one consistent system.
overview: The product had to feel premium while still being practical during real workout sessions.
The web app covers routine planning, matrixes, exercise libraries, videos, and workspace-level structure.
The mobile app brings that plan into the session with active routine progress, day detail, and exercise playback.
highlights: Cross-platform product thinking across web, iOS, and Android.
A strong visual direction with a dark editorial look and orange action cues.
Product flows that connect planning, playback, sharing, and progression instead of treating them as separate tools.
capabilities: Calendar and matrix views for training structure
Exercise and video libraries for session clarity
Mobile progress tracking for active plans

fit2go.name: Fit2Go
fit2go.tagline: Gamifying a healthier weekly meal planning flow.
fit2go.summary: Fit2Go is a prepared-meal ordering product built around weekly meal plans, nutrition targets, and pre-order delivery. My contribution focused on improving and gamifying the plan-selection experience.
overview: Fit2Go offers high-protein, low-calorie prepared meals with weekly ordering and subscription flows.
The product helps users choose plans based on goals, then preorder meals for the week with nutrition already computed across calories, carbs, and protein.
My role was focused on improving how users figure out what they should order based on factors like weight, age, allergies, food preferences, and spice tolerance.
highlights: I was brought in to improve one key product flow, not to build the full platform.
The main challenge was reducing friction in a decision-heavy meal planning experience.
The solution direction was to make the process feel more guided, more playful, and easier to finish.
rolePoints: Improved the calculator and recommendation journey around dietary goals and personal inputs.
Helped shape a more gamified experience so users could move through meal-plan selection with less drop-off.
Focused on the part of the journey where users estimate what and how many meals they may need in a week.
contextPoints: Users need help deciding based on body metrics and food constraints, not just by browsing meals manually.
The product includes customization around allergens, ingredients, and dietary preferences.
Because meals are pre-ordered and scheduled, the recommendation step has a strong effect on conversion.
